prd.oneLiner: A workplace asset-management system that connects cataloging, checkout and return, inventory audits, maintenance context, and operating reports.
prd.goal: Replace manual asset ledgers and reduce the time required for regular inventory audits by 50% within three months of launch.
prd.whyNow: More laptops, monitors, and shared equipment are being tracked across spreadsheets and chat, causing lost assets, unclear ownership, and duplicate purchases.
prd.problem: Operators cannot immediately tell where an asset is or who uses it, while employees cannot easily check availability or their return date.
prd.solution: Provide one source of truth for asset registration and QR identity, status, location and ownership, checkout and return, inventory audits, and operations reporting.
prd.alternatives: Spreadsheets, paper checkout ledgers, chat requests, and manual inventory counts.
prd.differentiator: Connect the asset list to its real checkout lifecycle, audit outcome, and maintenance context so operations decisions have evidence.

targets.name: Asset operator
targets.needs: Accurately manage asset location, condition, assignee, and checkout history.
targets.pain: Inventory counts and overdue follow-up are manual, and asset status is unreliable.

targets.name: Employee
targets.needs: Quickly find, request, and return the equipment they need.
targets.pain: They must ask an operator whether equipment is available and when it is due back.

scenarios.text: An employee finds an available projector for an event, requests it, and checks it out with a QR code after approval.
An operator records an asset whose location differs during a scheduled inventory audit.

prd.northStar: Share of active assets with a verified record

kpis.name: Asset-ledger accuracy
kpis.method: Matched assets ÷ audited assets in regular inventory counts

kpis.name: Checkout handling time
kpis.method: Measure time from request creation to approval or rejection

inScope: Asset registration, QR identity, status, location, and assignment
Checkout requests, approval, pickup, return, and overdue follow-up
Inventory audits, condition correction, and maintenance context
Asset, checkout, overdue, and audit operations reporting
nonGoals: Mobile device management or remote device control
assumptions: Employee and organization data are available through company SSO.
Each asset can receive a unique asset number or QR label.
risks: Inaccurate initial records may reduce operator trust.
Damaged QR labels or missing scan devices may disrupt field operations.
openQuestions: Which asset classes can be checked out without approval?
How will overdue alerts and recovery be operated?
constraints: Assignment and location changes must create audit logs.
Employees can view only their own loans and assets marked as discoverable.

requirements.title: Asset source of truth
requirements.desc: Operators identify every asset and view its current state.

acceptance.text: Each asset has a unique identifier, category, state, location, and owner or borrower.

features.title: Asset registration and QR identity
features.desc: Register asset information with a QR code or asset number.

acceptance.text: Duplicate asset numbers cannot be saved and required properties are validated.

specs.title: Core asset details
specs.desc: Record category, maker, model, asset number, and acquisition date.

acceptance.text: An operator can register an asset after entering the required properties.

features.title: Asset state and location lookup
features.desc: View available, checked-out, under-repair, and retired assets with their locations.

acceptance.text: Users can filter the list by name, category, state, and location.

specs.title: Asset detail history
specs.desc: Show changes to state, location, and assignee.

acceptance.text: The detail page shows current state and the latest change time.

requirements.title: Checkout and return
requirements.desc: Employees request, receive, and return assets while operators manage exceptions.

acceptance.text: Checkout state changes validate availability and user permissions before they are recorded.

features.title: Checkout request and approval
features.desc: Employees request an asset with dates and purpose; an operator approves it.

acceptance.text: A checked-out asset cannot receive a duplicate request and requesters can see approval status.

specs.title: Loan duration and purpose
specs.desc: Enter start date, due date, purpose, and a request note.

acceptance.text: The due date cannot be earlier than the start date.

features.title: Pickup, return, and overdue follow-up
features.desc: Process pickup and return by QR scan or asset number and manage overdue loans.

acceptance.text: After a return, the asset immediately becomes available or marked for inspection.

specs.title: Overdue alerts
specs.desc: Notify the borrower and operator when a due date passes.

acceptance.text: The overdue list shows borrower, asset, due date, and elapsed days.

requirements.title: Inventory audit and operations analysis
requirements.desc: Operators compare the physical inventory to the ledger and monitor operations metrics.

acceptance.text: Audit outcomes and metrics are traceable to original asset history.

features.title: Inventory audit
features.desc: Create location or category audit lists and record matches by QR scan.

acceptance.text: Mismatches are classified as missing, location mismatch, or state mismatch.

specs.title: Audit correction
specs.desc: Record the observed state and location for operator review.

acceptance.text: Before and after values and the auditor are retained in an audit log.

features.title: Asset operations dashboard
features.desc: Aggregate asset state, checkout, overdue, and audit-accuracy metrics.

acceptance.text: Operators can filter metrics by period, category, and location.

specs.title: Operations report
specs.desc: Show checked-out, under-repair, overdue, and unverified assets.

acceptance.text: Each number displays its calculation period and basis.
